howItWorks.intro: This travel insurance calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Premium = Trip Cost x Base Rate x Age Multiplier x Duration Multiplier
Typical range: 4-12% of total trip cost
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Is travel insurance worth it?
faq.answer: Yes for trips over $3,000, international travel, adventure activities, or travelers over 60. The typical cost is 4-12% of your trip. It covers trip cancellation, medical emergencies abroad, lost luggage, and evacuations.

faq.question: What does Cancel For Any Reason (CFAR) cover?
faq.answer: CFAR reimburses 50-75% of your trip cost if you cancel for any reason, including just changing your mind. It costs 40-60% more than standard policies and must usually be purchased within 14-21 days of your first trip deposit.

faq.question: Is the Travel Insurance Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard travel planning formulas trusted by travelers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What total trip cost should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate total trip cost value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Travel Insurance Calculator?
sections.body: The Travel Insurance Calculator is a free online tool designed for travelers who need quick, accurate calculations in the travel planning space. By entering your total trip cost, trip duration, traveler age, you get instant results including estimated premium, cost as % of trip, medical coverage. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting estimated premium right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In travel planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by travelers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your trips with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your total trip cost and need to find the right estimated premium. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Total Trip Cost ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your total trip cost — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: trip duration, traveler age, coverage level. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your estimated premium, cost as % of trip, medical coverage. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your total trip cost before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both estimated premium and cost as % of trip — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your trips.
If you're unsure about your coverage level, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
